heading: Art. 119. Manslaughter
label: 919 Art. 119. Manslaughter
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)919(a)919(b)50:713(a).50:713(b).(Art. 119), .May 5, 1950, ch. 169, § 164 Stat. 140The word “named” is substituted for the word “specified”.
amendments: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)919(a)919(b)50:713(a).50:713(b).(Art. 119), .May 5, 1950, ch. 169, § 164 Stat. 140The word “named” is substituted for the word “specified”.

children.label: a
children.text: Any person subject to this chapter who, with an intent to kill or inflict great bodily harm, unlawfully kills a human being in the heat of sudden passion caused by adequate provocation is guilty of voluntary manslaughter and shall be punished as a court-martial may direct.
while perpetrating or attempting to perpetrate an offense, other than those named in clause (4) of (article 118), directly affecting the person;section 918 of this title
